finding: The config-YAML template, the sandbox/consent/public_base_url branch, the update_check/bio_mcp/control_mcp/voice sections, and test_helpers are copied byte-for-byte into the shim's plan_spawn + test_helpers; only interpolated inputs are re-sourced from SpawnFacts. Diff vs pre-move blob = mechanical de-coupling only.

G2 foot-gun: `git grep CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR sdk/crates/ziee-test-harness` = 0. All 4 former env! sites (worktree key, repo-root .ziee-cache walk, migration roots, binary walk) take a runtime manifest_dir param fed by the CONSUMER's env! at the shim's TestHarness::new. Verified the shim seeds PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")).

`git grep ziee:: sdk/crates/ziee-test-harness` = 0. The 3 former ziee:: couplings resolved: init_storage_key + Windows helper → HarnessApp::before_spawn (shim impl); the file_routing doc-comment stays in the shim's data_dir(). Crate names ONLY the HarnessApp seam.

Risk-3 (runtime Variant vs compile-time is_desktop): the shim keeps is_desktop() via env!(CARGO_PKG_NAME) (still #[path]-compiled per crate → correct) and seeds Variant::{Server,Desktop}. template_db_base + migration_dirs match on Variant, preserving the exact desktop-template extra-migration split. Desktop test binary → Variant::Desktop → ziee_test_template_desktop + [server merged, desktop] dirs.

The pre-move 3 app tempdir fields (_workspace_tempdir, _sandbox_cache_tempdir Arc, _hub_tempdir) collapse into SpawnPlan.keep_alive: Vec<Box<dyn Any+Send+Sync>>, parked on SpawnedServer and dropped at test end. TempDir + Arc<TempDir> are Send+Sync+'static → bound satisfied. Same drop-order semantics (all reaped when the handle drops). The generic _data_tempdir stays engine-owned.

Drop for SpawnedServer: process.kill()+wait(), remove temp config, then spawn (on the ambient tokio Handle) terminate-backends + DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS. Byte-identical to pre-move except admin_db_url is a struct field (own copy) instead of re-reading DATABASE_URL via module fn — same value, and robust to a mid-run env change.

No build.rs, no sqlx `macros` feature (features=[postgres,runtime-tokio-rustls,migrate]). All compile-time query!/query_as! stay in the shim's test_helpers (which the ziee crate build DB satisfies). `cargo check -p ziee-test-harness` with no DB present = 0. Lean dev-dep, mirrors ziee-build-support.

Dep versions + feature sets copied from src-app/Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]: sqlx 0.8.6, tokio 1.47.1 (rt/sync/time subset), reqwest 0.12 (rustls-tls/charset/http2, default-features=false), uuid 1.18, url 2.5, tempfile 3.15, portpicker 0.1. Feature-union unifies onto the single src-app/Cargo.lock without a duplicate build (cargo check --workspace green).

Dev/test-only move: nothing in this chunk touches server routes, schemars types, or aide registrations — the harness is test-support code linked only by test binaries. The 4 generated files (openapi.{ui,desktop}.json + types.{ui,desktop}.ts) are untouched in the working tree (git status clean for them). Golden = trivially BYTE-IDENTICAL; no regen needed. Confirmed no generated file staged.

Zero-churn surface: TestServer keeps pub fields base_url/database_name/database_url (real fields on the shim struct, so `server.database_url` field access compiles) + methods api_url/data_dir + start/start_desktop/start_with_options with identical signatures. TestServerOptions keeps all 16 fields. test_helpers verbatim. The ~288 call sites + both #[path] includes need no edit.

The desktop test binary #[path]-includes the same shim file as `mod common` and uses crate::common::TestServer::start_desktop(). Added ziee-test-harness to desktop [dev-dependencies] (it previously relied on ziee-build-support being reachable). start_desktop → use_desktop_binary=true → binary_name ziee-desktop + --headless, while Variant::Desktop (from the desktop crate's PKG_NAME) drives the desktop template — the two independent signals preserved exactly.

No secret-handling change: the test jwt.secret + secrets.storage_key literals stay in the shim's app-side config (never in the SDK crate). init_storage_key stays app-side via before_spawn. The SDK engine handles only connection strings it is given (DATABASE_URL) — no new egress, no injected data-path. test_helpers RBAC SQL (groups/user_groups) unchanged.

Public surface: Variant, DbConn, SpawnFacts, SpawnPlan, HarnessApp (assoc type Options + template_db_base/migration_dirs/before_spawn/plan_spawn/health_path), TestHarness::{new,start}, SpawnedServer{pub base_url/database_name/database_url, data_dir()}, and re-export worktree_db. A ~10-line second-app impl reuses all machinery — the reuse posture the skeleton-server guard enforces. before_spawn + health_path have sensible defaults so a minimal app implements only 3 methods.
